channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Corporate Suites' 13-Year Lease: Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in the Deal
item.description: Corporate Suites signs 13-year, 34,857-SF lease at 16 E. 34th St in Midtown. The term length reveals how time, not rent, is the scarce resource in office leasing.

news:news.news:title: Corporate Suites' 13-Year Lease: Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in the Deal
news:news.news:keywords: office leasing, Midtown Manhattan, flexible workspace, lease duration, George Comfort & Sons, Corporate Suites

item.title: A Tenant Renewed 41,000 SF. The Lender Should Care.
item.description: Grassi renews 41,000 SF in Jericho, NY. The lease signals how tenant credit quality, not occupancy, is driving office underwriting for lenders.

news:news.news:title: A Tenant Renewed 41,000 SF. The Lender Should Care.
news:news.news:keywords: office lease, Jericho, Grassi, tenant credit, underwriting, Long Island

item.title: Lee & Associates' $5M Retail Loan Shows Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in Small-Balance CRE
item.description: Lee & Associates arranges $5M permanent loan for a 13,000-sf retail center near Providence. The 30-year amortization reveals how lenders use time to manage risk in small-balance CRE.

news:news.news:title: Lee & Associates' $5M Retail Loan Shows Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in Small-Balance CRE
news:news.news:keywords: retail financing, small-balance CRE, permanent loan, Lee & Associates, First Tech Federal Credit Union, Kempner Properties, Providence retail, amortization

item.title: A Hospital Sold a Vacant Building. Here's What the Buyer Had to Believe.
item.description: Kings Capital pays $30.1M for vacant FiDi building, plans $60M conversion with $41M Derby Copeland loan. No tax exemptions. The underwriting math is the story.

news:news.news:title: A Hospital Sold a Vacant Building. Here's What the Buyer Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: Kings Capital, New York Presbyterian Hospital, Derby Copeland, office conversion, FiDi, construction loan, market-rate housing

item.title: What HUD's Funding Shift Tells Lenders About Fair Housing Risk
item.description: Fair housing groups sue HUD over grant changes that could curtail investigations. For CRE lenders, the shift signals a change in enforcement risk and compliance cost.

news:news.news:title: What HUD's Funding Shift Tells Lenders About Fair Housing Risk
news:news.news:keywords: fair housing, HUD, regulation, enforcement risk, multifamily lending

item.title: SL Green's Guidance Looks Like a Demand Story. It's a Supply Story.
item.description: SL Green raised full-year guidance by 26% as Manhattan office leasing surged. The real driver: shrinking supply from conversions and no new construction, not a demand boom.

news:news.news:title: SL Green's Guidance Looks Like a Demand Story. It's a Supply Story.
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Manhattan office, leasing, supply, earnings guidance, office market

item.title: 999 Raymond St. Sale: The Buyer Is Underwriting Renovation, Not Location
item.description: Entre Commercial Realty brokers sale of 95,179 SF industrial facility in Elgin, IL. Buyer plans substantial renovations. Analysis of underwriting margin in secondary industrial.

news:news.news:title: 999 Raymond St. Sale: The Buyer Is Underwriting Renovation, Not Location
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Elgin, Brennan Investment Group, renovation, underwriting margin

item.title: What a 98-Unit Sale in Slingerlands Tells Owners About Secondary-Market Liquidity
item.description: ACP's sale of Meadowbrook Apartments in Slingerlands, NY, shows how secondary-market apartment liquidity depends on sponsor patience and basis discipline.

news:news.news:title: What a 98-Unit Sale in Slingerlands Tells Owners About Secondary-Market Liquidity
news:news.news:keywords: Adirondack Capital Partners, Meadowbrook Apartments, Slingerlands, multifamily, secondary market, liquidity, basis, capital markets

item.title: Pebb Capital's $69M South Beach Buy Shows Office Debt Is Available Only at the Right Basis
item.description: Pebb Capital buys South Beach's Uno office building with a $69M loan from ACORE Capital. The deal shows debt is available for the right basis and sponsor, not for all office.

news:news.news:title: Pebb Capital's $69M South Beach Buy Shows Office Debt Is Available Only at the Right Basis
news:news.news:keywords: Pebb Capital, ACORE Capital, South Beach, office, acquisition financing, Miami, Todd Rosenberg

item.title: Bond Markets Are Financing Student Housing Again. Here Are the Terms.
item.description: Radnor Property Group closed $147M in tax-exempt bonds for student housing in Atlanta. The deal shows how public debt is financing development private capital avoids.

news:news.news:title: Bond Markets Are Financing Student Housing Again. Here Are the Terms.
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, tax-exempt bonds, Atlanta, Radnor Property Group, development finance

item.title: Grocery-Anchored Retail Has a Price. This Deal Found It.
item.description: Kiemle Hagood's $32M sale of 5 Mile Shopping Center in Spokane reveals how grocery-anchored retail is now pricing on lease duration, not location. A capital markets analysis.

news:news.news:title: Grocery-Anchored Retail Has a Price. This Deal Found It.
news:news.news:keywords: grocery-anchored retail, Spokane, retail investment sales, capital markets, basis reset

item.title: ESRT's 1359 Broadway Sale: The Basis Is the Exit, Not the Asset
item.description: ESRT markets 1359 Broadway for $225M. At $462/sf with no debt, the sale reveals how REITs are recycling capital into basis-advantaged acquisitions, not just selling into a recovery.

news:news.news:title: ESRT's 1359 Broadway Sale: The Basis Is the Exit, Not the Asset
news:news.news:keywords: ESRT, 1359 Broadway, office sales, capital recycling, New York office, REIT strategy, basis

item.title: $153M in Orders for a $153M Deal: Affordable Housing CMBS Has a Bid
item.description: Systima Capital Management closed a $153M tax-exempt CMBS deal backed by 1,272 LIHTC units. The offering drew $1.2B in orders from 19 investors, signaling a structural shift in affordable housing capital.

news:news.news:title: $153M in Orders for a $153M Deal: Affordable Housing CMBS Has a Bid
news:news.news:keywords: CMBS, affordable housing, LIHTC, Systima Capital Management, J.P. Morgan

item.title: Nomura's $719M SASB Deal Shows Private Credit Is Choosing Multifamily Over Agency Debt
item.description: Nomura's $719M SASB CMBS refinances Keller's multifamily portfolio, signaling private credit's growing role over agency debt for large sponsors.

news:news.news:title: Nomura's $719M SASB Deal Shows Private Credit Is Choosing Multifamily Over Agency Debt
news:news.news:keywords: Nomura, SASB CMBS, multifamily, private credit, Keller Investment Properties, Newmark, refinancing

item.title: The Groundbreaking Looked Like a Start. It Was a Capital Stack Test.
item.description: Bonaventure breaks ground on $85.3M Norfolk multifamily project using HUD 221(d)(4) loan and opportunity zone equity. The capital stack reveals how developers are financing new supply in a high-rate environment.

news:news.news:title: The Groundbreaking Looked Like a Start. It Was a Capital Stack Test.
news:news.news:keywords: Bonaventure, HUD 221(d)(4), multifamily development, opportunity zone, Norfolk

item.title: HUD Is Still Lending. This Deal Proves It.
item.description: Dwight Capital closes $66M HUD 221(d)(4) construction loan for 312-unit project in Abilene, Texas. The deal reveals agency debt's structural advantage in a frozen bank market.

news:news.news:title: HUD Is Still Lending. This Deal Proves It.
news:news.news:keywords: HUD lending, multifamily construction, Dwight Capital, 221(d)(4), secondary markets

item.title: Greystone's $46.8M Fannie Mae Loan: Agency Debt Is Buying Time for Subsidized Housing
item.description: Greystone's $46.8M Fannie Mae loan for a Section 8 complex in Long Beach, NY, shows agency debt is underwriting subsidy duration. The 10-year term and 5-year IO reveal the capital pressure.

news:news.news:title: Greystone's $46.8M Fannie Mae Loan: Agency Debt Is Buying Time for Subsidized Housing
news:news.news:keywords: Fannie Mae, Section 8, affordable housing, agency debt, Metropolitan Realty Group

item.title: A Credit Committee Approved This Industrial Ground-Up. Here's What They Had to Believe.
item.description: Formation Interests and Crescent Real Estate break ground on 261,168 SF Phase II in Goodyear, AZ. A lender's-eye view of speculative industrial risk allocation in 2026.

news:news.news:title: A Credit Committee Approved This Industrial Ground-Up. Here's What They Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: industrial development, Phoenix, speculative construction, capital allocation, ground-up financing

item.title: BLP Bought a 782,775-SF Warehouse. Here’s What the Seller Had to Believe.
item.description: Bridge Logistics Properties buys a 782,775-SF distribution facility in Frederickson, WA. The deal shows how industrial basis is set when the developer needs liquidity and the buyer controls the terms.

news:news.news:title: BLP Bought a 782,775-SF Warehouse. Here’s What the Seller Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, basis, Bridge Logistics Properties, Panattoni, Seattle-Tacoma, Fred310

item.title: PMG's Society Brooklyn Shows Lease-Up Time Is Now the Most Expensive Ingredient in Development
item.description: PMG's Society Brooklyn opens with 500+ units and resort amenities. The real story is how lease-up time, not construction cost, now defines development risk in Gowanus.

news:news.news:title: PMG's Society Brooklyn Shows Lease-Up Time Is Now the Most Expensive Ingredient in Development
news:news.news:keywords: PMG, Society Brooklyn, Gowanus, lease-up, development risk, amenity arms race, New York multifamily

item.title: Core Spaces Sells Itself a Portfolio: The Basis Is the Structure, Not the Price
item.description: Core Spaces closes $300M+ student housing portfolio for CUL REIT. The transaction reveals how sponsors use non-listed REITs to lock in basis and manage liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Core Spaces Sells Itself a Portfolio: The Basis Is the Structure, Not the Price
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, non-listed REIT, Core Spaces, CUL REIT, capital stack

item.title: What a $30M Retail Portfolio Sale Tells Lenders About Basis Discipline
item.description: CBRE arranges $30M sale of 148,405-sf retail portfolio in Long Beach, CA. At 78% leased, the deal tests how lenders underwrite vacancy in a rate-constrained market.

news:news.news:title: What a $30M Retail Portfolio Sale Tells Lenders About Basis Discipline
news:news.news:keywords: retail, Long Beach, CBRE, portfolio sale, basis discipline, lease-up risk, Turnbridge Equities

item.title: Can Fair Housing Enforcement Survive a 95% Grant Cut?
item.description: HUD cuts fair housing grants from 100+ to 5, imposing a $5M minimum that disqualifies most local groups. The Massachusetts Fair Housing Center loses $425K of its $750K budget.

news:news.news:title: Can Fair Housing Enforcement Survive a 95% Grant Cut?
news:news.news:keywords: HUD, fair housing, grant cuts, enforcement, nonprofit funding

item.title: Helaba Exits Sutton Foreclosure: The Debt Was Never the Asset
item.description: Helaba exits foreclosure on Jeff Sutton's $50M Herald Square mortgage, assigning it to an SL Green-linked entity. The move reveals a capital structure trade, not a settlement.

news:news.news:title: Helaba Exits Sutton Foreclosure: The Debt Was Never the Asset
news:news.news:keywords: Helaba, Jeff Sutton, SL Green, foreclosure, Herald Square, retail debt, mortgage assignment, New York City

item.title: Can a $2.7M Waterfront Mansion in Oxnard Rewrite the Pricing Map for Coastal California?
item.description: A $2.7M Oxnard mansion with a private boat dock raises a question: how much of coastal California's premium is location vs. liquidity?

news:news.news:title: Can a $2.7M Waterfront Mansion in Oxnard Rewrite the Pricing Map for Coastal California?
news:news.news:keywords: Oxnard, waterfront, pricing, basis, secondary market, California, liquidity

item.title: 34,564 SF of Leasing at 711 Third: The Lease Duration Is the Signal, Not the Square Footage
item.description: SL Green signs 34,564 SF in two deals at 711 Third Avenue. Fidelity's 10-year lease and Ackman Ziff's renewal show tenants are committing to duration, not just space. The underwriting margin is time.

news:news.news:title: 34,564 SF of Leasing at 711 Third: The Lease Duration Is the Signal, Not the Square Footage
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, 711 Third Avenue, Fidelity National Title, Ackman Ziff, Midtown East, office leasing, lease duration, tenant commitment, underwriting margin

item.title: Blackstone's Retail Funds Look Like Access. They Are a Liquidity Trade.
item.description: Blackstone, Wellington, and Vanguard launch retail funds with a 3% redemption limit. The structure reveals the core tension: private assets don't scale to daily liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's Retail Funds Look Like Access. They Are a Liquidity Trade.
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, retail investors, private equity, real estate, liquidity, fund structure, Wellington, Vanguard

item.title: KREF Is Not for Sale. Its Loan Book Is.
item.description: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust posted a $122M loss and launched a strategic review. The real story is what the review reveals about liquidity, loan book composition, and the return of bank competition.

news:news.news:title: KREF Is Not for Sale. Its Loan Book Is.
news:news.news:keywords: KREF, KKR, commercial real estate lending, strategic review, loan portfolio, liquidity, bank competition, private credit

item.title: Mamdani's EDC Picks Signal Time Is the City's Most Expensive Development Input
item.description: NYC Mayor Mamdani names Anthony Shorris CEO and Lina Khan chair of EDC after 7-month delay. The vacancy cost developers time, and time is the most expensive ingredient in any capital stack.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's EDC Picks Signal Time Is the City's Most Expensive Development Input
news:news.news:keywords: NYC EDC, Zohran Mamdani, Anthony Shorris, Lina Khan, development timeline, public-private partnerships, affordable housing

item.title: JPMorgan's Third Avenue Exit: The $425 Million Consensus on Office Risk
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management seeks to sell its 49% stake in 605 Third Avenue at a $425M valuation. The price reveals how office capital is pricing vacancy, repositioning risk, and corridor bifurcation.

news:news.news:title: JPMorgan's Third Avenue Exit: The $425 Million Consensus on Office Risk
news:news.news:keywords: JPMorgan, Fisher Brothers, 605 Third Avenue, office recapitalization, New York City office

item.title: SEBCO's 1883 Crotona Avenue Completion Shows Senior Housing Capital Is Tied to Income Ceilings, Not Demand
item.description: SEBCO completes 134-unit senior housing in the Bronx. 92 units capped at 130% AMI. The basis works only because the income ceiling, not demand, sets the rent floor.

news:news.news:title: SEBCO's 1883 Crotona Avenue Completion Shows Senior Housing Capital Is Tied to Income Ceilings, Not Demand
news:news.news:keywords: senior housing, affordable housing, AMI, Bronx, SEBCO, Magnusson Architecture, capital stack, basis

item.title: Multifamily Debt Grew 53%. The Clock Is What Matters Now.
item.description: Multifamily loan balances at U.S. banks jumped 53% since 2019, adding $229 billion. The real pressure is the maturity wall those loans now face.

news:news.news:title: Multifamily Debt Grew 53%. The Clock Is What Matters Now.
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, bank lending, loan balances, maturity wall, CRED iQ

item.title: Bascom Buys Orange County Apartments for $53M: A 30-Year Hold Meets a Value-Add Thesis
item.description: Bascom Group pays $53.1M for a 183-unit Orange County complex. The $290K per-unit basis and renovation plan reveal a capital strategy built on operational control, not market timing.

news:news.news:title: Bascom Buys Orange County Apartments for $53M: A 30-Year Hold Meets a Value-Add Thesis
news:news.news:keywords: Bascom Group, Orange County, multifamily, value-add, basis, renovation, Newcastle, Institutional Property Advisors

item.title: Can a 980,000-SF Office Campus Trade When the Tenant Is the Only Bid?
item.description: King Realty Advisors and Colliers sell 217-acre Emerson/Copeland campus in St. Louis. Analysis of basis, single-tenant risk, and what the deal says about office liquidity.

news:news.news:title: Can a 980,000-SF Office Campus Trade When the Tenant Is the Only Bid?
news:news.news:keywords: office, St. Louis, single-tenant, basis, liquidity, Emerson, Copeland, Blackstone

item.title: Wawa Sold for $1,576 a Foot. That Price Tells the Story.
item.description: Marcus & Millichap brokered the $8.8M sale of a 5,585-sf Wawa in Edison, NJ. At $1,576/sf, the price reveals the capital story behind single-tenant net-lease retail.

news:news.news:title: Wawa Sold for $1,576 a Foot. That Price Tells the Story.
news:news.news:keywords: Wawa, single-tenant net-lease, retail investment sales, Marcus & Millichap, Edison New Jersey

item.title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Capital Allocation Question Behind the Odd Couple
item.description: Mayor Mamdani taps McKinsey partner Anthony Shorris and ex-FTC chair Lina Khan to lead NYC's EDC. The pairing reveals a capital allocation tension between development and antitrust scrutiny.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Capital Allocation Question Behind the Odd Couple
news:news.news:keywords: NYC EDC, Anthony Shorris, Lina Khan, Zohran Mamdani, economic development, antitrust, public finance, real estate policy

item.title: Pulte Is Buying Land, Not Office. That Is the Signal.
item.description: Finmarc sells 14 acres in Chantilly, VA to Pulte Homes for $26.4M. The office/flex building is being demolished for 183 homes. A capital markets signal about land value vs. office income.

news:news.news:title: Pulte Is Buying Land, Not Office. That Is the Signal.
news:news.news:keywords: Finmarc, Pulte Homes, office-to-residential, land value, Northern Virginia, Chantilly, Cushman & Wakefield, capital markets

item.title: $133M Providence Place Mall Sale: The Price of a Court-Ordered Exit
item.description: Providence Place Mall sells for $133M after years in receivership. The price reveals the discount required to clear a court-ordered sale and what it signals for regional mall liquidity.

news:news.news:title: $133M Providence Place Mall Sale: The Price of a Court-Ordered Exit
news:news.news:keywords: Providence Place Mall, Pyramid Management Group, Brookfield Properties, retail distress, receivership sale, regional mall pricing

item.title: A 10-Year Lease at $85 PSF Just Tested What Office Tenants Will Pay for Stability
item.description: Fidelity National Title Insurance signs 20K SF lease at SL Green's 711 Third Avenue. The 10-year term at Midtown East's average $85 PSF reveals the underwriting margin tenants now demand.

news:news.news:title: A 10-Year Lease at $85 PSF Just Tested What Office Tenants Will Pay for Stability
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Fidelity National Title, 711 Third Avenue, office leasing, Midtown East

item.title: New Mexico SIC Is Hiring. The Real Signal Is What It Already Owns.
item.description: New Mexico SIC seeks a real estate and real asset analyst. The hire signals portfolio complexity and manager oversight needs, not a new allocation mandate.

news:news.news:title: New Mexico SIC Is Hiring. The Real Signal Is What It Already Owns.
news:news.news:keywords: New Mexico SIC, sovereign wealth fund, real estate analyst, institutional capital, portfolio management, manager oversight

item.title: KREF Is Not Just Selling. It Is Admitting the Model Broke.
item.description: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust posts $122M loss, explores sale. The strategic review signals more than distress: it reveals the structural limits of floating-rate CRE lending in a reset market.

news:news.news:title: KREF Is Not Just Selling. It Is Admitting the Model Broke.
news:news.news:keywords: KREF, KKR Real Estate Finance Trust, CRE lending, strategic alternatives, floating-rate debt, loan portfolio, distress, liquidity

item.title: JPMorgan's $425M Third Avenue Exit: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Building
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management seeks to sell its 49% stake in Fisher Brothers' 605 Third Avenue at a $425M valuation, down from a $600M ask in 2020. The deal tests whether office capital is returning or just concentrating.

news:news.news:title: JPMorgan's $425M Third Avenue Exit: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Building
news:news.news:keywords: JPMorgan, Fisher Brothers, 605 Third Avenue, office recapitalization, New York City office market

item.title: Can a $2.7M Oxnard Mansion Prove Waterfront Value Exists Outside the Premium Bidding War?
item.description: A $2.695M Oxnard mansion with a private boat dock lists at a fraction of comparable LA or Miami waterfront. The basis gap signals where capital is rotating for yield.

news:news.news:title: Can a $2.7M Oxnard Mansion Prove Waterfront Value Exists Outside the Premium Bidding War?
news:news.news:keywords: waterfront real estate, Oxnard, basis gap, secondary market, lifestyle asset, capital rotation

item.title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Clock on City Land Deals Just Got More Expensive
item.description: Mayor Mamdani names Anthony Shorris CEO and Lina Khan chair of NYCEDC after a 7-month delay. The appointment signals a shift in how city land deals are structured, compressing the timeline for developers.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Clock on City Land Deals Just Got More Expensive
news:news.news:keywords: NYCEDC, Anthony Shorris, Lina Khan, Zohran Mamdani, city land deals

item.title: 25.3 Acres of Concrete Paved the Way for This Laredo IOS Deal
item.description: Realterm buys 40-acre IOS facility in Laredo, TX. The 25.3 concrete-paved acres and Heartland Express lease reveal how infrastructure scarcity drives industrial outdoor storage pricing.

news:news.news:title: 25.3 Acres of Concrete Paved the Way for This Laredo IOS Deal
news:news.news:keywords: industrial outdoor storage, Realterm, Laredo, Heartland Express, transit-oriented properties

item.title: The Plaza at Normandy Sale: A 1031 Buyer Paid Full Basis for a 2024 Build
item.description: TSCG arranges $20.7M sale of Publix-anchored shopping center in Jacksonville. A 1031 buyer pays full basis for a 2024-vintage, fully leased asset. What this signals about retail capital demand.

news:news.news:title: The Plaza at Normandy Sale: A 1031 Buyer Paid Full Basis for a 2024 Build
news:news.news:keywords: Publix, Jacksonville, 1031 exchange, grocery-anchored retail, TSCG

item.title: Can a $300 Million Infrastructure Check Buy What a Pension Fund Really Needs?
item.description: Maine PERS commits $300M to Brookfield Super-Core Infrastructure Partners. The allocation reveals how pension funds are trading liquidity for yield as time becomes the most expensive ingredient in infrastructure capital.

news:news.news:title: Can a $300 Million Infrastructure Check Buy What a Pension Fund Really Needs?
news:news.news:keywords: Maine PERS, Brookfield, infrastructure, pension funds, capital allocation

item.title: Maroa Park Sale: The Fresno Multifamily Bid Is Real, but Narrow
item.description: JBT Property Management sells 248-unit Maroa Park Apartments in Fresno for $44 million. The trade reveals a narrow bid for multifamily in secondary markets.

news:news.news:title: Maroa Park Sale: The Fresno Multifamily Bid Is Real, but Narrow
news:news.news:keywords: Fresno, multifamily, JBT Property Management, Mogharebi Group, private buyer

item.title: State of Utah Buys Franklin Campus: The Buyer Is the Only Buyer That Matters
item.description: Utah pays $40M for Franklin Campus at $128/sf. The state's cost of capital and mission-driven demand reveal a new pricing tier in office.

news:news.news:title: State of Utah Buys Franklin Campus: The Buyer Is the Only Buyer That Matters
news:news.news:keywords: office, government buyer, Utah, basis reset, public sector, Salt Lake City, FranklinCovey

item.title: Can a Golf Resort Sale Prove Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in CRE?
item.description: Ohana Real Estate Investors pays $191M for Panther National golf resort. The deal reveals how time, leverage, and execution risk shaped Centaur's exit and J.P. Morgan's underwriting.

news:news.news:title: Can a Golf Resort Sale Prove Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in CRE?
news:news.news:keywords: golf resort, Palm Beach Gardens, Centaur Holdings, Ohana Real Estate Investors, J.P. Morgan Chase, acquisition financing, master-planned development, time as cost of capital
